title: wyred contract v0 — retained external lock baseline (.baseline.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the retained EXTERNAL lock baseline, emitted only for LOCKED emits (intent_05a_pinned, mppt_2420_hc_reva, watchy_v1_reva, watchy_v1_revb). Source: harness/emit.py `snapshot_locks` (which returns {series, solver_version, groups}) PLUS the engine-added `connector_pinout` key (emit.py: `baseline["connector_pinout"] = [...]`, noting "extra keys are ignored by the harness gate"). This is F6: the golden four-key shape is snapshot_locks output plus one extra key; the harness snapshot_locks docstring (three keys) is the stale tier (goldens > code). All four keys always emitted. `groups` is a map <group name> -> {version, covers, entries}; a group's `entries` is the canonical covered allocation entries (empty when the group covers no pool_allocation decision class, e.g. the external-interface/connector_pinout group — F5). `connector_pinout` reuses the shared ICD row $def (empty array when no external-interface rows, e.g. intent_05a_pinned). additionalProperties:false throughout.

series.description: the design series letter this baseline was taken at (free string; "A"/"B" in goldens)

solver_version.description: the emitting solver version (free string, e.g. "ga019-trivial-1")

connector_pinout.description: the external-interface connector-pinout (ICD) rows retained alongside the snapshot; the decision class the harness allocation record does not materialize as entries (F6). Empty when the emit has no external-interface rows.

groups.description: lock groups keyed by name (snapshot_locks emits `groups` as a name -> group map, groups sorted by name in the serialization). Each value is the per-group frozen state.

baseline_group.description: One lock group's baselined state (emit.py `snapshot_locks`: {version, covers, entries}). Note this differs from common.defs.json#/$defs/lock_group (the l1/records declared-lock shape, which carries name/snapshot/owner/sync_point): here the name is the map key, there is no `snapshot`, and `entries` holds the canonical covered allocation entries. `entries` is [] when the group does not cover the pool_allocation decision class (F5: the external-interface/connector_pinout group is locked at version 1 with entries []). `covers` members are the closed DECISION_CLASSES vocabulary (mirrors lock_group.covers). All three keys always emitted.
